text:
Sean Gallagher: Heads up: trying to close out GA readiness for Dedicated Burst this week. Can folks reply w/ status on the final checklist items + any remaining blockers?

Sean Gallagher: Checklist (please confirm ✅ / 🚫):
1) Runbooks complete + linked from alerts (burst pool overload, circuit breaker, denial spikes)
2) Alerts tuned (noise acceptable) + paging policy agreed
3) Game day executed + outcomes documented
4) Rollback plan + kill switches validated (per-tenant, per-region/hw allowlist, global circuit breaker)
5) SLO regression gate enabled (auto-reduce/disable burst when non-burst p95 regresses)

Rafael Mendes: ✅ Runbook: burst pool overload mitigation is in place and reviewed w/ oncall. I added a “first 10 minutes” section + comms template to Support/CS.
Link: confluence/eng-sre/runbooks/burst-pool-overload-mitigation-runbook

Rafael Mendes: One note: we should add a short ...

text:
Allison Grant: hey all — need to lock initial alert thresholds for the new cache dashboards before we turn them on in prod. goal is “regression signal w/o paging on workload mix changes”.

Allison Grant: current candidate alerts:
- prefix cache hit rate drop
- KV cache reuse effectiveness drop
- eviction pressure sustained high
- wasted prefill ratio spike

Allison Grant: question: what should page vs ticket, and what dimensions do we alert on (global vs region vs model)?

Sean Gallagher: +1. we should be super conservative on paging for anything cache-efficiency related. it’s a guardrail SLO, not a customer SLA.

Aisha Bello: agree. hit-rate swings a *lot* with prompt length / traffic mix. if we page on “hit rate down” alone we’re going to hate ourselves.

Sergio Costa: fwiw I can wire 2 severities:
- SEV2 page (oncall)
- SEV4 ticket (triage in hours)

Sergio Costa: but need crisp pr...

title: #eng-sre: 1739995402
preview:
Sean Gallagher: Need to lock down the SLO regression gate semantics for burst before we call GA-ready. Specifically: what exactly trips the gate + what counts as a rollback trigger vs “auto-throttle burst”.

Sean Gallagher: Context: PR-23988 adds “disable/reduce burst when non-burst latency regresses”. We need agree...
text:
Sean Gallagher: Need to lock down the SLO regression gate semantics for burst before we call GA-ready. Specifically: what exactly trips the gate + what counts as a rollback trigger vs “auto-throttle burst”.

Sean Gallagher: Context: PR-23988 adds “disable/reduce burst when non-burst latency regresses”. We need agreed thresholds and windows so we don’t flap.

Peter Holtz: +1. Also routing needs deterministic behavior. If the gate trips mid-flight, we should be explicit: does it return BURST_DENIED (w/ reason) or do we hard-disable the whole region/hw class flag.

Jordan Lee: Current implementation (policy engine rules) supports 3 actions:
1) `burst_mode=normal`
2) `burst_mode=throttled` (reduce max burst ratio + tighten per-tenant token bucket refill)
3) `burst_mode=disabled` (deny all burst admissions)

Jordan Lee: It’s keyed by (region, hw_class) and can be overridden by global circu...

text:
Aisha Bello: for the quant profile canary next week, we need to lock alert thresholds. Specifically `fallback_rate` + any quality guardrail signals we’re emitting. What do we want to page on vs just warn?

Connor O'Brien: +1. canary is 1% traffic on 2 routes (Acme hosted + 1 dedicated). We need a “hit kill-switch” moment that’s unambiguous.

Logan Wright: can we align on metric names so we don’t build alerts on the wrong series? I’m seeing:
- `quant_profile.resolution_outcome` = applied|fallback|disabled
- `quant_profile.fallback_reason` = incompatible|load_error|policy_error|unknown
and then dashboard computes fallback_rate as fallback / (applied+fallback). Is that what we’re using?

Caleb Johnson: tracing side is tagging `quant_profile_id` + `resolution=applied|fallback|disabled`. For alerting we should use the aggregated counters not traces, but we can link traces in the runbook.

...

``` 
Job: redwood-private-control-plane-migrate
...
ERROR: canceling statement due to lock timeout
DETAIL: Process 21492 waits for ShareLock on transaction 778192; blocked by process 21301.
CONTEXT: while updating tuple (12,3) in relation "deployments"
STATEMENT: ALTER TABLE deployments ADD COLUMN rollout_policy jsonb;
```

Sahana Iyer: it retries once (k8s job backoff), then fails again. total run ~4m.

Michael Nguyen: that `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN` should be quick, but it does take an ACC...

Baseline (last ~12 weeks, Sev0-2 only):
- Deploy regressions (service-level): median MTTR ~58m, p90 ~2h05m

text:
Rafael Mendes: Dropping this here to unblock the v2 measurement section. We keep saying “MTTR reduction” but we need to agree on baseline + how we’re going to measure. Can folks sanity check:

Baseline (last ~12 weeks, Sev0-2 only):
- Deploy regressions (service-level): median MTTR ~58m, p90 ~2h05m
- Misconfig / routing-policy: median ~42m, p90 ~1h30m
- Capacity/saturation (regional): median ~1h12m, p90 ~3h10m

These are from PD resolved time - created time, then filtered to “prod” incidents tagged sev0/1/2.

Questions:
1) Do we want MTTR = time to mitigate (customer impact stopped) or time to resolve (all follow-ups done)?
2) How do we avoid gaming (e.g., resolve early, keep mitigation ongoing)?
3) What’s the “context completeness” score that we can track per incident thread?

Sean Gallagher: +1 we need to be super explicit. For reliability KPI we should use “time to mitigate” (impac...

title: #eng-sre: 1739121100
preview:
Rafael Mendes: DR game day outcomes + immediate fixes (Private backup/restore) — posting while it’s fresh. Thanks all for jumping in.

Rafael Mendes: Scenario we ran: control-plane corruption (config DB + partial k8s state) -> restore into clean cluster (same region) using latest nightly backup artifact + Helm/Terra...
text:
Rafael Mendes: DR game day outcomes + immediate fixes (Private backup/restore) — posting while it’s fresh. Thanks all for jumping in.

Rafael Mendes: Scenario we ran: control-plane corruption (config DB + partial k8s state) -> restore into clean cluster (same region) using latest nightly backup artifact + Helm/Terraform templates. Goal RTO: 60m, RPO: 15m.

Rafael Mendes: High level result:
- RPO: ✅ met for control-plane state (backup at T-9m).
- RTO: ❌ missed (restore-to-healthy took ~1h47m; root cause was preflight gaps + ordering + one KMS retry loop).

Sean Gallagher: +1. We basically lost ~30m to “unknown unknowns” in restore ordering and then another ~15m on chasing a false negative in the validation check.

Miles Thornton: For the record: this is a good failure mode to catch now. The core restore path worked, but our operator experience isn’t deterministic yet.

Rafael Mendes: T...

Miles Thornton: posting draft SLO proposal for provision-to-ready (fleet bring-up). want SRE eyes before we lock thresholds + wire alerts. doc is here: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/eng-sre/Provision-to-ready+SLO+draft (WIP)

Miles Thornton: tl;dr proposal
- SLI: time_to_ready = ts(Ready) - ts(Allo...
text:
Miles Thornton: posting draft SLO proposal for provision-to-ready (fleet bring-up). want SRE eyes before we lock thresholds + wire alerts. doc is here: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/eng-sre/Provision-to-ready+SLO+draft (WIP)

Miles Thornton: tl;dr proposal
- SLI: time_to_ready = ts(Ready) - ts(Allocated)
- SLO (steady state): P50 <= 25m, P95 <= 90m (per region + gpu_type), and success rate >= 98.5% (Ready within 2h)
- error budget: 30d window, standard burn rate alerts
- alerting: page for sustained burn (fast + slow), ticket for regional / gpu-specific regression

Aisha Bello: thanks. 2 q’s:
1) what’s the exact “Allocated” timestamp source of truth? terraform apply? cloud instance running? control plane “capacity allocated”? we’ve had weird gaps there.
2) pls don’t page on P50 drifting during rollout week. it’s going to be noisy.

Miles Thornton: 1) good call. draft...

title: #eng-sre: 1738702109
preview:
Sean Gallagher: Kicking off thread on SLO gates + paging policy for Dedicated burst. Goal: bursting must *never* silently blow up non-burst tenants.

Proposal: add an automated “non-burst protection gate” that can reduce/disable burst when neighbor SLOs regress. We should be able to ship this before beta expands.

K...
text:
Sean Gallagher: Kicking off thread on SLO gates + paging policy for Dedicated burst. Goal: bursting must *never* silently blow up non-burst tenants.

Proposal: add an automated “non-burst protection gate” that can reduce/disable burst when neighbor SLOs regress. We should be able to ship this before beta expands.

Key idea:
- We measure latency SLOs only on *non-burst* traffic (baseline Dedicated capacity lanes), per region/hwclass.
- If those degrade beyond threshold vs rolling baseline, we progressively clamp burst caps (or trip global burst circuit breaker) until recovery.
- Gate integrates with rollout flags (per region/hw class) + admission controller.

Open questions:
1) What exact SLI? p95 vs p99? request latency vs queueing?
2) What windows / deltas (absolute vs relative) are safe + not noisy?
3) Paging: when do we page SRE vs alert-only?

Rafael Mendes: +1. If this isn’t full...

text:
Sean Gallagher: We need to lock down *standard* gate thresholds + templates for Dedicated staged rollouts. Right now every rollout doc/thread has different numbers and we’re going to end up with noisy alerts + inconsistent promote decisions.

Sean Gallagher: Can we align on v1 defaults this week? At minimum: error rate, p95/p99 latency, saturation/headroom, OOM rate, 429/drop, GPU health. Plus: evaluation window + minimum sample size.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also want a clear stance on “per-customer variability” — we can’t tune per account in v1 or we’ll never scale oncall.

Peter Holtz: Agree. If we do fixed numbers that are too strict we’ll just stall rollouts forever (then operators force-promote and we lose safety). If too lax, gates won’t catch regressions. Need to tie to SLO + error budget.

Paula Mendes: (looping in) I’m drafting defaults for PM-827 but need SRE signoff. Question: ...

text:
Aisha Bello: seeing something weird in the Dedicated throughput SLO dashboard for streaming routes. tokens/sec looks ~1.8–2.2x higher than non-streaming for same deployment/model. feels like we’re double counting output tokens per request when streaming. anyone else seeing this?

Sergio Costa: yeah, I noticed the “tokens_out_per_s” panel spikes exactly when customers flip `stream=true`. query is based on `redwood.inference.tokens_out_total` / `redwood.inference.request_duration_seconds`. if tokens_out_total is inflated, the ratio goes nuts.

Sean Gallagher: +1 this will break the SLO evaluator if the canonical metric is tokens/sec. can we confirm what we emit for streaming? do we count per chunk or per request?

Logan Wright: there are two different paths:
- non-streaming: we emit `tokens_out` once on finalize
- streaming: we emit `tokens_out` *per chunk* today (because chunks are “re...

title: #eng-sre: 1738436709
preview:
Sean Gallagher: For the Private backup/restore dashboards (PR-94), what do we actually want to graph + alert on? I keep coming back to `last_success_ts`, `duration`, `bytes`, but we need to avoid paging during planned maintenance windows / upgrades where backups are intentionally paused.

Allison Grant: Yep, those a...
text:
Sean Gallagher: For the Private backup/restore dashboards (PR-94), what do we actually want to graph + alert on? I keep coming back to `last_success_ts`, `duration`, `bytes`, but we need to avoid paging during planned maintenance windows / upgrades where backups are intentionally paused.

Allison Grant: Yep, those are the right primitives.
For graphs, I’d do 4 panels:
1) “Time since last successful backup” (computed)
2) Duration p50/p95 (or last duration if it’s periodic)
3) Backup size bytes (last + trend)
4) Failure reasons (stacked by error_category)

Allison Grant: For alerting, avoid alerting directly on `success==0` (too noisy). Prefer “no success within expected interval”.
If we have `*_last_success_timestamp_seconds`, alert on `time() - last_success > (interval + grace)`.

Sergio Costa: Agree. In the metrics PR we’ve got:
- `redwood_private_backup_last_success_timestamp_second...

PagerDuty: [P1] Alert triggered: unit_econ_hourly_completeness_low (completeness=0.972 threshold=0.99) | service=unit-econ-pipeline | runbook=https://go.redwood/runbooks/unit-econ#completeness

Allison Grant: yeah saw that too. current rule is paging on *any* hourly completeness < 0.99 for any dimension combo. that’s basically guaranteed to flap when a region is late.

Aisha Bello: +1. we’re effectively alerting on the most granular series. we should alert on aggregate coverage first, then optionally surface drilldowns in the dashboard.

Logan Wright: agree. a single region lagging ingestion for 10 min shouldn’t page SRE. also worth noting we had a backfill running ~02:00 that delayed the hourly job.

Sean Gallagher: Heads up: proposing a game day next week to simulate *experiment config propagation lag* and verify alerts + runbooks + rollback steps before we broaden beta. Goal is to reproduce the staging “stale config” behavior on demand and make sure on-call muscle memory is there.

Aisha Bello: If we do this, I want clear “stop conditions” + a safety plan. We can do it in staging only, but we’ve had staging metrics changes spill over into shared alert channels.

title: #eng-sre: 1737765510
preview:
Sean Gallagher: starting a thread on SLOs + alert routing for the unit econ pipeline (freshness + completeness). goal: make Finance comfortable using blended $/1M output tokens in exec reporting without waking people up for noise.

Sean Gallagher: current mental model is 3 layers:
1) ingestion (metering events landi...
text:
Sean Gallagher: starting a thread on SLOs + alert routing for the unit econ pipeline (freshness + completeness). goal: make Finance comfortable using blended $/1M output tokens in exec reporting without waking people up for noise.

Sean Gallagher: current mental model is 3 layers:
1) ingestion (metering events landing)
2) hourly aggregation job (warehouse tables/views)
3) API/dashboard availability (query endpoints)

Sean Gallagher: proposal v0 (numbers up for debate):
- Freshness SLO: 99% of hours have complete aggregates available within 90 min of the hour boundary.
- Completeness SLO: 99.5% of hourly buckets have non-null {model, region, tier} AND output_tokens > 0 where requests > 0.
- Correctness: more tricky; for v1 use “reconciliation delta vs billing” as a weekly check + alert only on large deltas.

Sean Gallagher: alerting idea:
- warn (Slack) if freshness lag > 60 min for 2 ...

preview:

text:
Rafael Mendes: Dropping here for review: draft runbook for assignment debugging (route experiments). Goal is to cover “why is my split wrong / why is a user flipping variants / why are metrics missing” w/ copy-pastable checks. Please add any missing known-bad patterns.

Sean Gallagher: skimming now. Overall solid structure (symptom -> likely causes -> checks). One thing: call out “propagation lag” earlier. On-call keeps chasing hashing issues when it’s just stale config.

Allison Grant: +1. Also we should add a known-bad for “variant 0 gets 100% despite allocation” when evaluator falls back due to config fetch failure.

Jordan Lee: reading. A few nits + patterns we’ve seen:
- low-entropy stickiness key (defaulting to `ip` or empty string) leads to skew + churn
- mixing `customer_id` vs `session_id` across requests in same logical user (some SDKs)
- route id mismatch (raw path vs templ...

title: #eng-sre: 1737419905
preview:
Sean Gallagher: Need to lock SLOs + alerts for the audit log pipeline this week so we can ship dashboards + link from the runbook. What’s the proposal for (1) delivery latency and (2) drop rate? Also: do we differentiate Hosted vs Private?

Kevin Osei: For audit pipeline I’d like 2 things:
- ingestion->sink delivery...
text:
Sean Gallagher: Need to lock SLOs + alerts for the audit log pipeline this week so we can ship dashboards + link from the runbook. What’s the proposal for (1) delivery latency and (2) drop rate? Also: do we differentiate Hosted vs Private?

Kevin Osei: For audit pipeline I’d like 2 things:
- ingestion->sink delivery latency (p95)
- event loss (dropped/failed permanently)

Retries will cause duplicates so “error rate” alone isn’t enough. We should alert on “cannot deliver” and “buffer backlog” too.

Aisha Bello: +1. Draft SLO doc in slo-toolkit PR is up: https://github.com/redwood/slo-toolkit/pull/298
Right now it’s:
- Delivery latency SLO: 99.9% of audit events delivered within 5m (enterprise tiers), 15m (standard)
- Drop rate SLO: < 0.01% permanently failed per 30d window

Open Q: do we measure "delivered" at exporter ack vs stored in our canonical store?

Allison Grant: Canonical de...

title: #eng-sre: 1736965120
preview:
Allison Grant: flagging a concern before we merge more experiment tags into OTel/metrics. Cardinality risk is real here.

Allison Grant: Current proposal in ADR-048 + PRs is effectively adding `{route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, model_id, region}` to a bunch of metrics. For hosted API this can blow up fast (route...
text:
Allison Grant: flagging a concern before we merge more experiment tags into OTel/metrics. Cardinality risk is real here.

Allison Grant: Current proposal in ADR-048 + PRs is effectively adding `{route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, model_id, region}` to a bunch of metrics. For hosted API this can blow up fast (route_id alone is scary if it’s raw paths).

Sean Gallagher: +1. We’ve been burned by label explosions before. If we do this naively it’s going to page SRE and also cost $$$.

Logan Wright: agreed. our intent was attribution correctness, but we can probably be stricter about what goes to metrics vs traces/logs.

Caleb Johnson: the ingestion side can handle some increase, but not “unbounded per customer route + experiment ids + model ids”. also impacts rollups + storage.

Jordan Lee: route_id should be normalized route template, not raw path. We already have a “route template id”...

title: #eng-sre: 1736455521
preview:
Sean Gallagher: We need to lock alert thresholds + escalation policy for route experiments before we expand beta. Specifically: config propagation lag + attribution drop rate. Can we propose defaults today and get buy-in from Platform/Telemetry?

Rafael Mendes: yup. I took a first pass based on what we saw in stagin...
text:
Sean Gallagher: We need to lock alert thresholds + escalation policy for route experiments before we expand beta. Specifically: config propagation lag + attribution drop rate. Can we propose defaults today and get buy-in from Platform/Telemetry?

Rafael Mendes: yup. I took a first pass based on what we saw in staging last week. Main thing is we should alert on *lag*, not just “router had no config” since the cache masks it.

Allison Grant: +1. Also reminder from observability review: keep alert signals stable, avoid per-{route,experiment,variant} explosions. Alert should be at service/env level with drilldowns.

Rafael Mendes: Draft thresholds:
- Propagation lag (p95) from control plane write -> router seeing version.
 - Warn: > 60s for 10m
 - Page: > 180s for 5m
- Propagation lag (max) / “staleness bound exceeded”
 - Page: any instance reporting > 10m staleness for 2m (this implies w...
